title: fminit causes std::out_of_range
body:
### Version

0.54+15

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

With the following SystemVerilog design:
```systemverilog
module counter(input clk, input rst, output logic is_full);


    logic [1:0] ctr;
    always @(posedge clk) begin
        if (rst) begin
            ctr <= 0;
        end else begin
            ctr <= ctr+1;
        end
    end
    assign is_full = (ctr == 2'b11);

endmodule
```
run the following yosys script:
```
read_verilog -sv -formal reset.sv

hierarchy -check -top counter
prep -top counter
fminit -seq rst 0,1
```
The following output is given:
```
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: what():  vector::_M_range_check: __n (which is 1) >= this->size() (which is 1)
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: finished (returncode=-6)
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: task failed. ERROR
```

### Expected Behavior

Documentation of fminit is rather thin, but I would expect either an error about the sequence not being supported, or for the sequence to properly initialise.

### Actual Behavior

The aforementioned ``std::out_of_range`` is given. 

The error is not thrown if the sequence is 1,0, but re-appears if the sequence is 1,0,1 or similar. I think it's an issue from adding entries of the ``initdata`` vector in the ``fminit.cc`` pass, but am unsure about further details.
code_blocks:
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: what():  vector::_M_range_check: __n (which is 1) >= this->size() (which is 1)
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: finished (returncode=-6)
SBY 14:39:48 [reset_task1] base: task failed. ERROR
